evidence: Four specialist write agents hit authorization limits while targeting repositories outside the writable workspace.
cause: Write delegation happened before validating subagent filesystem permissions.
remediation: Pilot one agent after writable-scope preflight; keep external-workspace agents read-only and apply changes from the principal agent.

evidence: Eleven wait calls exhausted configured budgets totaling approximately 1510 seconds.
cause: Workers were polled without a strict progress and takeover gate.
remediation: Wait once for at most 60 seconds, inspect progress, and take over after two no-progress observations.

evidence: Three workers were manually closed or taken over after prolonged silence.
cause: Delegated writes could not complete in their permission scope.
remediation: Delegate read-only audits outside writable roots and reserve edits for writable worktrees.

evidence: The 100000-message benchmark timed out without producing a result.
cause: Full workload ran before calibration.
remediation: Run a one-percent benchmark calibration and estimate the full timeout before the production workload.

evidence: A late default change forced another full build and an unchanged benchmark was repeated.
cause: Defaults were finalized after expensive validation and default wiring was verified with a full run.
remediation: Finalize defaults before Docker and verify argument wiring with a tiny smoke workload.

evidence: Two apply_patch calls failed under the Windows sandbox.
cause: The patch tool could not enforce the configured split writable roots.
remediation: Stage inside one writable worktree and use one supported patch path before starting broad edits.

evidence: Exact replacement, interpolation, newline, path API, parser, and staging-path mistakes required repeat work.
cause: One-off PowerShell diagnostics were not syntax-checked or tested on a single repository first.
remediation: Syntax-check generated PowerShell and pilot every inventory transformation on one repository.

evidence: Forty-five commits were authored on the excluded date; the user attributed that work to Antigravity and OpenCode.
cause: User-owned external tool window.
remediation: Preserve this event for provenance and exclude it from Codex efficiency totals.

evidence: Three apply_patch attempts failed because the Windows sandbox rejected split writable roots.
cause: The current patch tool cannot prepare the configured Windows sandbox.
remediation: Use a staged single-root edit path and do not retry the same failing tool after the first confirmed platform failure.

evidence: The first 30-repository credential scan exceeded a 10-second command timeout; the retry completed.
cause: The timeout did not account for thirty Git process startups.
remediation: Budget repository-wide Git scans from a one-repository timing sample.

evidence: Two malformed git patches, one unterminated PowerShell edit command, and one unhandled native Git stderr path required correction.
cause: Fallback editing and native-command handling were not piloted with the smallest possible change.
remediation: Pilot one minimal edit and one failing native Git call before applying a workspace-wide script.

evidence: Portfolio report exceeded 60 seconds because validation spawned repeated Git processes for each evidence type.
cause: Tracked-file inventory was queried repeatedly instead of cached once per repository.
remediation: Use one git ls-files and one porcelain-v2 status snapshot per repository, then filter in memory.

evidence: The first cached but serial Git inventory still exceeded a 30-second timeout.
cause: Repository Git snapshots remained serial.
remediation: Collect read-only Git snapshots through a bounded cross-version runspace pool.

evidence: Two invalid Measure-Command invocations and one PowerShell-7-only parallel implementation were rejected.
cause: Runtime compatibility and command shape were not checked before timing.
remediation: Read PSVersion first and use Stopwatch plus a PowerShell-5.1-compatible runspace pool.

evidence: The GitHub CLI command was attempted but gh is not installed; the REST API fallback succeeded.
cause: Tool availability was not checked before invocation.
remediation: Use Get-Command preflight and call the existing REST client when gh is unavailable.

evidence: The first local git clone failed because the restricted Windows Git shell could not create its signal pipe.
cause: Local clone required the external-repository permission path.
remediation: Use the approved local-clone path on the first attempt for external repository sources.

evidence: A large inline PowerShell edit exceeded the initial 10-second timeout before writing.
cause: Command startup and parsing cost were not included in the timeout.
remediation: Use staged files and a 30-second floor for multi-file PowerShell edits.

evidence: Repeated Docker Desktop Go validation strategies timed out: bind-mount validator, go-test stage attempts, and named-cache attempt.
cause: Multiple uncalibrated strategies were tried after one direct Go container test had already passed; Windows-to-Linux compilation dominated.
remediation: Accept the first isolated passing test as evidence, avoid repeated equivalent runs, and move cross-platform cache design to a separate kit task.

evidence: A local git fetch used an abbreviated SHA as a remote ref and failed; the full SHA succeeded.
cause: Fetch semantics were not piloted.
remediation: Always resolve and use the full commit SHA for local-clone integration.

evidence: Two aggregate waits returned no completion while worktree status already showed active progress.
cause: Progress could be observed from worktrees without waiting for final agent messages.
remediation: Inspect disjoint worktrees and wait only when integration is blocked.

evidence: Two completed specialist agents reported three apply_patch sandbox failures each.
cause: The Windows split-root patch wrapper rejected their edits.
remediation: Pilot the patch wrapper once, then use one supported staged edit path without retrying the same failure.

evidence: The embeddings/LLM-eval specialist reported three pyenv shim hangs.
cause: Interpreter resolution was not preflighted.
remediation: Resolve the absolute interpreter once before running tests.

evidence: The embeddings/LLM-eval specialist reported two malformed incremental patches.
cause: Patch changes were split before validating the first patch shape.
remediation: Validate one complete patch on a single file before incremental fan-out.

evidence: report-portfolio.ps1 rejected OutputPath because the supported parameters are MarkdownPath and JsonPath.
cause: The command was issued before reading the script parameter block.
remediation: Read the parameter block before invoking repository scripts and reuse the corrected command.

evidence: A .NET regex replacement treated dollar-anchor text in PowerShell source as replacement tokens and corrupted only the writable staging copy; syntax validation caught it before integration.
cause: Source code was passed as a regex replacement string instead of using delimiter-based substring replacement.
remediation: Use index-delimited substring replacement for source blocks and parse PowerShell immediately after every generated edit.

evidence: merge-base could not resolve a commit that existed only in another clone object database.
cause: The commit was compared before fetching the local clone into the official repository.
remediation: Fetch the source clone first, then perform ff-only merge or compare FETCH_HEAD.

evidence: The new parser rejected pre-existing Markdown-style backtick scalars and the legacy indentation-specific catalog counter.
cause: Earlier validation checked YAML by text patterns instead of parsing it.
remediation: Keep PyYAML parsing in CI and count catalog entries independent of valid sequence indentation.

evidence: validate-kit attempted to parse sync-catalog-stacks.py with the PowerShell parser.
cause: A global list insertion added a Python script to the PowerShell-only validation list.
remediation: Maintain language-specific validation lists and parse generated scripts immediately.

evidence: Local-path git clone failed in Git for Windows because sh.exe could not create a signal pipe.
cause: The cloning strategy was selected without a Windows local-transport preflight.
remediation: Prefer a short-path git worktree for local repository isolation on Windows.

evidence: Worktree checkout failed with filename-too-long errors because 111 .gradle cache files are tracked.
cause: Repository hygiene was not checked before creating a normal-length Windows worktree.
remediation: Preflight tracked build caches with git ls-files, reject them in validation, and use a short C:\tmp worktree for cleanup.

evidence: Copying templates/validate-project.ps1 into the sparse staging tree failed because the templates parent directory had not been created.
cause: The overlay staging directory was treated as a full clone during a new file-family edit.
remediation: Create destination parents for every sparse-overlay copy or use the official full clone after switching to an isolated branch.
